summary: Iteration 17 (multi-sport player profile + backwards-compat regression) — Seeded 2 players directly in mongo (Player A +919900111111 multi-sport target, Player B +919900222222 legacy cricket-only with NO interested_sports). Drove the full UI scenario end-to-end via Playwright. ALL 13 STEPS PASSED: (1) login as Player A via /players/login [player-login-submit], (2) chip picker shows exactly 10 sports, (3) clicking football/chess/cricket chips renders sport-section-football/chess/cricket dynamically, (4) filled football position=midfielder, preferred_foot=left, jersey=10, (5) filled chess rating=2000, title=IM, preferred_color=black, (6) filled cricket role=all-rounder, batting=right, bowling=right-arm-fast, (7) Save triggered 'Profile saved' toast, (8) reload /players/me — all 3 sections repopulated with the exact values (jersey=10, rating=2000, midfielder, left, all-rounder, right-arm-fast), (9) sport-remove-cricket removed the cricket section AND flipped the cricket chip back to unselected (no inline background-color style), (10) re-saved successfully, (11) on /players/profiles searching 'QA Multi' and opening their public profile shows player-sport-cards with player-card-football + player-card-chess and NO player-card-cricket — football card text 'midfielder|POSITION|left|PREFERRED FOOT|10|JERSEY NUMBER' and chess card 'CHESS|2000|RATING|IM|TITLE|black|PREFERRED COLOR' confirm only filled fields rendered, (12) for the legacy player B (no interested_sports in mongo) public profile shows exactly ONE player-card-cricket containing all legacy values (batsman / left / right arm spin / 99), (13) logging in as legacy player and visiting /players/me — the cricket chip is AUTO-SELECTED (inline backgroundColor #10B981 confirmed via getAttribute) and the cricket section is pre-populated with role=batsman, batting=left, bowling=right-arm-spin, jersey=99 (withLegacyMigration helper verified). Backend PATCH /players/me persisted interested_sports + sport_profiles + mirrored legacy cricket fields without any errors (no 500s in backend log). Existing console 401s come from /api/auth/me on the public profile route while not logged in (expected — these are pre-existing and not a regression).
critical_code_review_comments: PlayerProfile.jsx withLegacyMigration helper does not handle the edge case where a player has BOTH interested_sports (non-empty) AND legacy cricket fields filled — in that case the legacy cricket values are silently ignored on first read. Currently low risk since seed/signup flows initialise interested_sports=[], but worth a guard if you ever import data from a third-party.

Public profile route /players/profiles is gated by auth — anonymous visitors get redirected to login. If the product intent is 'public player directory' this should be opened up; if it's intentionally private it is fine. Documented as a behavioural note, not a bug.

PlayerDirectory.jsx SportCards renders every key inside sport_profiles[sport] but does not skip the schema's `default` value the way the editor does — so a player who selects football but never touches preferred_foot will end up with 'right' rendered on their public card after first save (because the editor coalesces value || default before sending). Consider stripping default-equivalent values server- or client-side before display to keep 'shows only filled fields' literally true.

PlayerProfile.jsx save() mirrors cricket.* into legacy top-level fields but never deletes the legacy fields when the player removes the cricket chip — so after step 9 the user's cricket legacy fields (role/batting_hand/etc.) remain in mongo even though sport_profiles.cricket may also be empty. Confirmed via behaviour: the public profile correctly hides the cricket card because interested_sports no longer includes 'cricket', but the stale legacy fields linger in the DB. Minor data-hygiene nit, not a functional bug.

SportField in SportProfileSection.jsx calls `field.default || field.options[0]` even for non-select types — defensive, but the number/text/textarea branches don't read .default at all, so the dead-code path is harmless. Tidy-up opportunity.

success_rate.backend: 100% (login + GET /api/players/me + PATCH /api/players/me + GET /api/players/profiles + GET /api/players/profiles/{id} all return 200 with correct data shapes)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (13/13 UI steps including the chip toggle, dynamic per-sport sections for football/chess/cricket, save+reload persistence, remove + chip flip, public profile rendering for both multi-sport and legacy players, and legacy auto-migration on /players/me)

test_credentials: Seeded: player A mobile +919900111111 / password test123 (email 919900111111@example.com, name QA Multi). Player B mobile +919900222222 / password test123 (email 919900222222@example.com, name QA Legacy). Both have email_verified=true and role=player. Note: initial seed used @qa.test domain which fails Pydantic EmailStr ('special-use or reserved name') — switched to @example.com. Seed script at /app/test_reports/seed_players.py is idempotent (deletes prior runs first).
seed_data_creation: Two test users + two player_profiles inserted into mongo (test_database / collections users + player_profiles). Player A has interested_sports=['football','chess'] + sport_profiles populated for football+chess (cricket sport_profile section was removed via UI). Player B has legacy cricket fields only (role=batsman, batting_hand=left, bowling_style=right-arm-spin, jersey_number=99) and NO interested_sports key — exactly mirrors a pre-multi-sport profile.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Multi-sport player profile feature is GREEN. Key data-testids: interested-sports-picker (10 chips with data-testid='interested-sport-{slug}' for slug in cricket|football|basketball|badminton|tabletennis|volleyball|chess|quiz|hackathon|other), sport-section-{slug}, sport-remove-{slug}, sp-{slug}-{fieldKey} per the SPORT_SCHEMAS in /app/frontend/src/lib/sportProfileSchema.js, pp-save for the Save button, player-sport-cards container with player-card-{slug} children on the public directory profile, player-search-q/player-search-btn/player-search-card-{id} for searching. The player-login button is 'player-login-submit' (NOT 'login-submit'). The 'selected chip' state is detectable via the inline backgroundColor style (set when chip is on). withLegacyMigration in PlayerProfile.jsx auto-fills sport_profiles.cricket from top-level legacy fields when interested_sports is empty and any legacy cricket field has a non-default value. /players/profiles requires player auth to browse (returns redirect to /players/login when cookies are cleared) — keep at least one cookie set when navigating to that route in future tests. SendGrid is broken at the account level (401) — ignore those errors. Seed script at /app/test_reports/seed_players.py is reusable.
rca of the issue: n/a — no defects found. Both the new multi-sport flow and the legacy backwards-compat path work as specified. The only failure during execution was a test-script issue (test cleared cookies before searching the directory, which is auth-gated); the test was adjusted to stay logged in for the directory search, and that path then passed cleanly.
